patches.rationale: The brief explicitly states the product should expose bounded Jira business capabilities instead of raw tools.
patches.value: Provide a governed Jira fronting experience that lets agents access bounded Jira context and prepare or request Jira actions without exposing raw Jira APIs or MCP tools.

patches.rationale: This captures the core business risk the governed fronting layer is intended to solve.
patches.value: Agents need Jira support that is safe and usable, but direct Jira APIs and MCP-backed operations expose raw tool surfaces, unbounded access patterns, and write paths that can bypass review, visibility limits, and policy controls.

patches.rationale: These goals are directly supported by the governance posture and user-provided brief.
value: Give agents bounded, actor-visible Jira issue and backlog context for read-only work.
Replace raw Jira tool access with governed business capabilities.
Ensure write-adjacent Jira actions stop at preview or explicit approval before execution.
Record audit evidence for governed write-adjacent requests and approvals.
Trigger clarification when required inputs are missing instead of guessing.

patches.rationale: The source provides concrete, stable Jira user task families that the product should support.
This summarizes the stated governance posture in PM-readable behavior terms.
patches.value: The product should return only bounded, actor-visible Jira context for reads; never expose raw Jira tools directly; prepare previews for write-adjacent actions; require explicit approval when policy requires execution; and ask clarifying questions when required inputs are missing.

patches.rationale: The brief clearly distinguishes allowed reads from approval-gated write-adjacent actions.
patches.value: Read-only Jira context can proceed within bounded visibility limits. Write-adjacent actions must stop at preview or approval before execution, with policy checks and audit evidence recorded for governed requests.

patches.rationale: The brief is specific enough on purpose, governance, and approval posture to support making the Product Summary lockable after review.

envelope.summary: Draft PM-facing product summary patch candidates derived from the Jira governed fronting brief. These focus on bounded Jira access, preview-first write behavior, explicit approval stops, and auditability.
envelope.title: Business Summary Proposal: Jira Governed Fronting Gate Clean
watchouts: The source mixes template guidance with a user-provided brief; prioritize the brief where wording differs.
Supported question families should stay at stable user-task level, not mirror internal capability names.
Do not frame preview-only preparation as direct Jira mutation.

next_steps: Confirm the real business role names that should replace or validate the minimal Jira-specific actor labels below.
Confirm whether one requester actor is sufficient or whether support, engineering, and release work need separate actors.
Identify the explicit approver role or roles for governed execution requests.
After role confirmation, accept the actor patches and refine any actor-specific visibility or approval differences.

patches.rationale: The brief clearly describes a user-facing governed Jira surface with bounded reads and preview-first or approval-gated write-adjacent actions, but does not name a concrete business role. A minimal requester actor captures those expectations without inventing unsupported org-specific titles.

value.action_expectations: Can search bounded backlog context, inspect issue context, and prepare previews for stories, subtasks, incident bugs, customer-safe comments, release notes, assignee changes, sprint moves, workflow transitions, and issue links within the governed surface.
value.approval_expectations: Can proceed directly with bounded read actions. For write-adjacent actions, should stop at preview or submit a governed request when approval is required by policy rather than executing changes directly.
value.notes: This is a minimal requester actor derived from the brief because no concrete business role names were provided. Replace with source-true role names if the product serves a more specific audience such as support, engineering, or release operations.
value.summary: Business user who works through the governed Jira fronting surface to inspect bounded Jira context and prepare Jira changes without direct access to raw Jira tools.
value.title: Jira Requester
value.visibility_expectations: Can view bounded Jira issue and backlog context limited to actor-visible fields, comments, history, and allowed project or board scope. Should not see raw JQL, raw REST or MCP tools, or unbounded issue export.

patches.rationale: The governance posture explicitly says some write-adjacent actions require explicit approval and audit evidence. An approver actor is therefore warranted, but its exact scope should be confirmed rather than invented.

value.action_expectations: Can review requests for governed Jira actions such as workflow transitions, sprint moves, assignee changes, issue links, or other write-adjacent operations that require explicit approval.
value.approval_expectations: Acts as the approval boundary for policy-gated Jira actions, but the brief does not specify whether this authority is universal or varies by action type.
value.notes: Approval ownership is underspecified in the brief. Confirm whether one approver role is sufficient or whether different Jira actions route to different approvers.
value.summary: Business actor who reviews governed Jira execution requests and associated audit evidence before policy-gated changes proceed.
value.title: Jira Approver
value.visibility_expectations: Can inspect the prepared Jira change preview, relevant bounded context, policy signals, and audit evidence needed to approve or reject a governed request.

questions_for_user: Who is the primary business actor that uses bounded Jira read capabilities such as issue context and backlog context?

Should the same actor be allowed to request write-adjacent previews for comments, subtasks, stories, incident bugs, assignee changes, sprint moves, workflow transitions, issue links, and release notes, or do some of these belong to different business roles?
Who has approval authority for governed Jira execution requests when policy requires approval?
Does approval authority differ by action type, for example workflow transitions versus assignee changes versus sprint moves?
Should release notes preparation be available to the same actor who works issues, or to a separate release-oriented actor?
Are customer escalation comment previews prepared by the same Jira user role, or by a support or incident-facing role that should be modeled separately?
envelope.summary: Proposes PM-facing actor entries for a governed Jira fronting service. The brief is clear on bounded read access and approval-gated write-adjacent actions, but it does not explicitly name business roles, so the patches use minimal Jira-specific actors and flag approval ownership gaps for confirmation.
envelope.title: Actor Model Patch Proposal: Jira Governed Fronting
watchouts: The source describes capability boundaries well but does not name concrete business roles, so actor splits beyond a minimal set would be speculative.
Do not infer Jira permission schemes or backend principals as business actors.
Approval is required for some write-adjacent actions, but the approving role is not identified in the brief.
Several prepare-only capabilities may belong to different teams in practice; combining them into one actor may be too broad if the product is used across support, engineering, and release workflows.

next_steps: Review whether these business areas match durable PM language for Jira access, governed changes, and auditability.
Confirm whether audit evidence should stand alone or stay embedded in governed action handling.
Accept the patch candidates you want promoted into the business areas artifact.

patches.rationale: The brief explicitly allows read-only issue and backlog context while prohibiting raw REST or MCP exposure, making bounded Jira context a durable business area.

value.description: Bounded read-only access to Jira issue and backlog context with actor-visible scope and without exposing raw query or tool surfaces.
value.label: Jira Context Access

patches.rationale: The brief groups multiple write-adjacent Jira actions under a common governance pattern: stop at preview or approval rather than direct execution.

value.description: Preparation, preview, and approval-gated Jira change actions such as comments, subtasks, stories, incident bugs, assignee changes, sprint moves, workflow transitions, issue links, and release notes.
value.label: Governed Jira Change Requests

patches.rationale: Governance posture is central to the intent: required approvals, clarification instead of guessing, and audit evidence recording are cross-cutting responsibilities that merit a stable PM-facing area.

value.description: Explicit approval handling, stop conditions, clarification on missing inputs, and audit evidence capture for governed Jira operations.
value.label: Approval and Audit Controls

questions_for_user: Do you want read-only Jira context and governed change requests tracked as separate business areas, or grouped under one Jira fronting area?
Should audit evidence be its own business area or remain part of governance and approvals?
Do you expect release note preparation to live with Jira change preparation or under a separate reporting-oriented area?
envelope.summary: Draft PM-facing business area patch candidates derived from the Jira fronting brief. These areas separate bounded Jira access from governed write-adjacent controls and audit expectations.
envelope.title: Business Areas Proposal: Jira Governed Fronting Gate Clean
watchouts: Several listed capabilities came from starter guidance, so business areas should reflect the explicit brief and governance posture rather than template taxonomy.
Avoid naming business areas after implementation details like REST, MCP, or fronting service internals.
If approvals, previews, and audit evidence are critical policy boundaries, collapsing them into a generic Jira area may weaken downstream policy design.

next_steps: Confirm canonical actor ids and business-area ids in Product Design.
Decide which Jira actions are preview-only versus approval-required.
After vocabulary is available, add rule-level permission patches for bounded reads and approval-gated write-adjacent actions.
Review whether audit evidence needs to be captured in Permission Intent notes or in a downstream policy artifact.

patches.rationale: This captures the core trust posture stated in the brief without inventing actor or business-area bindings.
patches.value: Expose governed Jira business capabilities instead of raw REST or MCP tools. Allow bounded read-only issue and backlog context. Require write-adjacent Jira actions to stop at preview or explicit approval, return clarification when required inputs are missing, and record audit evidence for governed actions.

questions_for_user: What canonical actor ids should these Jira permission rules bind to? The current vocabulary is empty, so actor-specific rules cannot be added schema-validly yet.
What canonical business-area ids should these Jira permission rules use? The current vocabulary is empty, so scoped permission rules cannot be added schema-validly yet.
Should all write-adjacent Jira actions share one approval posture, or do some actions stop at preview only while others require explicit approval to proceed?
Who is allowed to view bounded Jira issue and backlog context: all supported actors, or only a subset?
envelope.summary: Draft permission-intent patch candidates based on the Jira governed fronting brief. The brief clearly defines bounded read access and preview-or-approval stops for write-adjacent Jira actions, but canonical actor and business-area ids are not yet available, so rule-level permission patches should wait for vocabulary clarification.
envelope.title: Permission Intent Proposal: Jira Governed Fronting Gate Clean
watchouts: Do not add permission rules with invented actor ids or business-area ids; the schema requires canonical vocabulary once rules are created.
The source text is a starter-template draft, so capability names and governance language are useful signals but should still be confirmed as product intent.
The brief distinguishes bounded reads from write-adjacent actions; collapsing them into one posture would lose an important business control.
Release notes are listed with write-adjacent actions but described as prepare-only; avoid treating them as direct Jira mutation.

next_steps: Confirm the initial capability set and cut anything not needed for MVP.
Define actor visibility and approval policy by action type and Jira scope.
Specify bounded read payloads for backlog context and issue context, including excluded fields.
Define the preview contract for each write-adjacent action and the approval handoff behavior.
Agree on audit evidence requirements and browser UI verification scenarios for acceptance.

items.body: The product shall present Jira as a bounded ANIP capability surface for agents and browser users. It shall not expose raw Jira REST endpoints, raw MCP tools, or unrestricted low-level operations directly. Capability entry points shall use business-oriented intents such as issue context, backlog context, and governed change requests.
items.rationale: This is the clearest core intent in the brief and defines the product boundary.
items.title: Expose Jira through governed business capabilities rather than raw tools

items.body: The product shall support read-only access to Jira issue context and backlog context within allowed Jira scope. Returned data shall be limited to actor-visible, bounded business context rather than unbounded exports or arbitrary query access. The system shall prevent raw JQL exposure and other mechanisms that could bypass bounded retrieval.
items.rationale: The brief explicitly allows read-only issue and backlog context while prohibiting unbounded access patterns.
items.title: Allow only bounded read-only Jira context

items.body: For write-adjacent Jira actions, the product shall stop before direct mutation unless an explicit approval path is satisfied. This applies to comments, subtasks, stories, incident bugs, assignee changes, sprint moves, workflow transitions, issue links, and release notes. The system shall return a preview or approval-ready request rather than silently executing the Jira change.
items.rationale: The brief names the action families and states they must stop at preview or approval.
items.title: Gate all write-adjacent Jira actions at preview or approval

items.body: When a requested Jira capability lacks required inputs, policy context, or target scope, the product shall ask for clarification instead of guessing defaults or inferring unsafe values. Clarification prompts should identify the missing business input needed to continue the governed flow.
items.rationale: The governance posture explicitly says missing required inputs should trigger clarification instead of guessing.
items.title: Require clarification when required inputs are missing

items.body: The product shall evaluate each read and write-adjacent request against actor-visible Jira scope and applicable approval policy before returning results or advancing a request. Where policy requires approval, the system shall stop at the approval boundary and present the request in a reviewable form. Where scope or policy denies access, the system shall not reveal out-of-scope Jira data.
items.rationale: The brief repeatedly emphasizes actor-visible bounds and approval-controlled actions.
items.title: Enforce actor-visible scope and approval policy on every governed action

items.body: The product shall record audit evidence for write-adjacent previews, approval-required requests, and policy-driven stop conditions. Audit evidence should capture enough context to show what was requested, what Jira scope and policy were applied, what preview was produced, and why execution did or did not proceed.
items.rationale: The brief explicitly requires audit evidence for write-adjacent actions.
items.title: Record audit evidence for previews, approvals, and governed stops

items.body: The first release shall support browser-verifiable flows that demonstrate the governed fronting behavior end to end. Verification should show that users can access bounded Jira context, receive previews for write-adjacent actions, encounter approval stops where required, and observe that raw Jira tools are not exposed through the UI.
items.rationale: The project summary calls out clean browser UI verification, but exact acceptance criteria are not yet specified.
items.title: Support clean browser UI verification of governed Jira fronting

questions_for_user: Which Jira actor types must be supported first, and do their visibility rules differ by project, board, or issue type?
What approval authority is required for each write-adjacent action: comments, subtasks, stories, incident bugs, assignee changes, sprint moves, workflow transitions, issue links, and release notes?
What audit evidence must be recorded for previews and approvals, and where must that evidence be retained?
Should backlog search and issue context support only a fixed allowlist of projects and fields, or can admins configure scope later?
What browser UI verification outcomes define success for the first release of the fronting gate?
envelope.summary: Candidate PM requirement blocks derived from the Jira governed fronting brief. Focus is on bounded Jira capabilities, no raw tool exposure, and approval/audit controls for write-adjacent actions.
envelope.title: Requirement Proposal: Jira Governed Fronting Gate Clean
watchouts: The source includes starter-template guidance; some listed capabilities may not all belong in the first release.
'Governed' and 'Product-first gating' imply policy decisions that are not yet fully specified by actor, project, or action.
Read boundaries are clear at a high level, but the exact field-level limits for issue context, comments, and history are not defined.
Write-adjacent flows stop at preview or approval, but execution ownership after approval is not described.
Audit requirements are stated, but retention, schema, and reviewer access expectations are still unclear.

next_steps: Select the scenario candidates that belong in the first PM draft.
Confirm the primary actor set and approval model so role-specific outcomes can be made concrete.
Choose the top 3-5 Jira capabilities for v1 scenario detail.
Define the bounded read scope for issue context and backlog context.
Add explicit audit expectations for preview, approval, and policy-stop flows.

The service design should state that policy checks, approval stops, and audit evidence are part of the capability outcome, not hidden backend behavior.

envelope.title: Success Criteria Patch Proposal: Jira Governed Fronting Gate Clean
watchouts: The source is partly starter-template guidance, so criteria should emphasize the user-provided brief over template completeness.

Write-adjacent criteria should verify preview or approval stops, not successful mutation execution.
If audit evidence is required, success criteria should name reviewable artifacts rather than vague logging claims.

bundle.sourceText:
# Source 1: Jira governed fronting intent
Kind: Fronting intent
Filename: jira-fronting-intent.md

# Jira Governed Fronting Intent

> Imported from starter template `jira-fronting-starter`. Treat this as draft guidance, not discovered truth.

Expose a governed Jira capability surface in front of native API or MCP-backed operations.

## Template-suggested capabilities
- jira.backlog.search_context: Find bounded work context in an allowed Jira project without exposing raw JQL or unbounded issue export.
- jira.issue.get_context: Inspect one Jira issue with bounded actor-visible fields, comments, and history.
- jira.incident_bug.prepare: Prepare a Jira bug ticket from incident or defect context without creating it.
- jira.story.prepare: Prepare a Jira story preview with acceptance criteria before any issue is created.
- jira.subtask.prepare: Prepare a subtask under an existing parent issue without creating it.
- jira.customer_escalation.comment.prepare: Prepare a customer-safe Jira comment from incident or triage context without posting it.
- jira.workflow_transition.request: Request a governed Jira issue transition and stop for approval when policy requires it.
- jira.sprint_move.request: Request moving Jira issue(s) into a sprint or backlog with approval and actor-visible board scope.
- jira.assignee_change.request: Request reassignment of a Jira issue with backend user resolution, preview, approval, and audit.
- jira.issue_link.request: Request linking two Jira issues with a declared relationship and cross-project policy checks.
- jira.release_notes.prepare: Prepare release notes from bounded Jira issues without publishing or mutating Jira.

## Governance posture
- Raw Jira tools are not exposed directly to agents.
- Reads return bounded actor-visible context.
- Write-adjacent actions prepare previews or require explicit approval before execution.
- Missing required inputs trigger clarification instead of guessing.

## User-provided project brief
Create a governed ANIP fronting service for Jira. Agents should see bounded Jira business capabilities, not raw REST or MCP tools. Read-only issue and backlog context is allowed. Write-adjacent actions such as comments, subtasks, stories, incident bugs, assignee changes, sprint moves, workflow transitions, issue links, and release notes must stop at preview or approval and record audit evidence.
bundle.summary: Review this source-derived Product Design bundle, answer only blocking clarifications, and save accepted sections into deterministic Studio artifacts.
bundle.title: Product Design Draft: Jira Governed Fronting Gate Clean

data.approval_posture_summary: Read-only Jira context can proceed within bounded visibility limits. Write-adjacent actions must stop at preview or approval before execution, with policy checks and audit evidence recorded for governed requests.
business_goals: Give agents bounded, actor-visible Jira issue and backlog context for read-only work.
Replace raw Jira tool access with governed business capabilities.
Ensure write-adjacent Jira actions stop at preview or explicit approval before execution.
Record audit evidence for governed write-adjacent requests and approvals.
Trigger clarification when required inputs are missing instead of guessing.
data.business_problem: Agents need Jira support that is safe and usable, but direct Jira APIs and MCP-backed operations expose raw tool surfaces, unbounded access patterns, and write paths that can bypass review, visibility limits, and policy controls.
data.governed_behavior_summary: The product should return only bounded, actor-visible Jira context for reads; never expose raw Jira tools directly; prepare previews for write-adjacent actions; require explicit approval when policy requires execution; and ask clarifying questions when required inputs are missing.
data.product_purpose: Provide a governed Jira fronting experience that lets agents access bounded Jira context and prepare or request Jira actions without exposing raw Jira APIs or MCP tools.

actors.action_expectations: Can search bounded backlog context, inspect issue context, and prepare previews for stories, subtasks, incident bugs, customer-safe comments, release notes, assignee changes, sprint moves, workflow transitions, and issue links within the governed surface.
actors.approval_expectations: Can proceed directly with bounded read actions. For write-adjacent actions, should stop at preview or submit a governed request when approval is required by policy rather than executing changes directly.
actors.notes: This is a minimal requester actor derived from the brief because no concrete business role names were provided. Replace with source-true role names if the product serves a more specific audience such as support, engineering, or release operations.
actors.summary: Business user who works through the governed Jira fronting surface to inspect bounded Jira context and prepare Jira changes without direct access to raw Jira tools.
actors.title: Jira Requester
actors.visibility_expectations: Can view bounded Jira issue and backlog context limited to actor-visible fields, comments, history, and allowed project or board scope. Should not see raw JQL, raw REST or MCP tools, or unbounded issue export.

actors.action_expectations: Can review requests for governed Jira actions such as workflow transitions, sprint moves, assignee changes, issue links, or other write-adjacent operations that require explicit approval.
actors.approval_expectations: Acts as the approval boundary for policy-gated Jira actions, but the brief does not specify whether this authority is universal or varies by action type.
actors.notes: Approval ownership is underspecified in the brief. Confirm whether one approver role is sufficient or whether different Jira actions route to different approvers.
actors.summary: Business actor who reviews governed Jira execution requests and associated audit evidence before policy-gated changes proceed.
actors.title: Jira Approver
actors.visibility_expectations: Can inspect the prepared Jira change preview, relevant bounded context, policy signals, and audit evidence needed to approve or reject a governed request.

entries.description: Bounded read-only access to Jira issue and backlog context with actor-visible scope and without exposing raw query or tool surfaces.
entries.label: Jira Context Access

entries.description: Preparation, preview, and approval-gated Jira change actions such as comments, subtasks, stories, incident bugs, assignee changes, sprint moves, workflow transitions, issue links, and release notes.
entries.label: Governed Jira Change Requests

entries.description: Explicit approval handling, stop conditions, clarification on missing inputs, and audit evidence capture for governed Jira operations.
entries.label: Approval and Audit Controls

data.policy_summary: Expose governed Jira business capabilities instead of raw REST or MCP tools. Allow bounded read-only issue and backlog context. Require write-adjacent Jira actions to stop at preview or explicit approval, return clarification when required inputs are missing, and record audit evidence for governed actions.

rules.notes: Studio-derived review candidate because the assistant produced a policy summary but no concrete actor-by-business-area rules. Confirm or edit before locking Product Design.

entries.evidence: Negative-path review shows incomplete or policy-sensitive requests trigger visible clarification or denial behavior with no inferred mutation-ready output.
entries.statement: When required inputs are missing or policy checks block progress, the product asks for clarification or stops explicitly instead of guessing.

entries.evidence: PM or compliance review can inspect audit artifacts for representative governed actions and confirm they capture the request, preview or approval boundary, and resulting decision state.
entries.statement: Approval-gated and preview-based Jira flows produce reviewable audit evidence for what was requested, what was shown, and why execution did or did not proceed.

actors.action_expectations: Can search bounded backlog context, inspect issue context, and prepare previews for stories, subtasks, incident bugs, customer-safe comments, release notes, assignee changes, sprint moves, workflow transitions, and issue links within the governed surface.
actors.approval_expectations: Can proceed directly with bounded read actions. For write-adjacent actions, should stop at preview or submit a governed request when approval is required by policy rather than executing changes directly.
actors.notes: This is a minimal requester actor derived from the brief because no concrete business role names were provided. Replace with source-true role names if the product serves a more specific audience such as support, engineering, or release operations.
actors.summary: Business user who works through the governed Jira fronting surface to inspect bounded Jira context and prepare Jira changes without direct access to raw Jira tools.
actors.title: Jira Requester
actors.visibility_expectations: Can view bounded Jira issue and backlog context limited to actor-visible fields, comments, history, and allowed project or board scope. Should not see raw JQL, raw REST or MCP tools, or unbounded issue export.

actors.action_expectations: Can review requests for governed Jira actions such as workflow transitions, sprint moves, assignee changes, issue links, or other write-adjacent operations that require explicit approval.
actors.approval_expectations: Acts as the approval boundary for policy-gated Jira actions, but the brief does not specify whether this authority is universal or varies by action type.
actors.notes: Approval ownership is underspecified in the brief. Confirm whether one approver role is sufficient or whether different Jira actions route to different approvers.
actors.summary: Business actor who reviews governed Jira execution requests and associated audit evidence before policy-gated changes proceed.
actors.title: Jira Approver
actors.visibility_expectations: Can inspect the prepared Jira change preview, relevant bounded context, policy signals, and audit evidence needed to approve or reject a governed request.

entries.description: Bounded read-only access to Jira issue and backlog context with actor-visible scope and without exposing raw query or tool surfaces.
entries.label: Jira Context Access

entries.description: Preparation, preview, and approval-gated Jira change actions such as comments, subtasks, stories, incident bugs, assignee changes, sprint moves, workflow transitions, issue links, and release notes.
entries.label: Governed Jira Change Requests

entries.description: Explicit approval handling, stop conditions, clarification on missing inputs, and audit evidence capture for governed Jira operations.
entries.label: Approval and Audit Controls

entries.rationale: The brief defines a governed Jira capability surface with bounded business capabilities rather than native tool access.
entries.statement: Do not expose raw Jira REST, MCP tools, or raw JQL directly to agents through this fronting surface.

entries.rationale: Allowed reads are limited to bounded backlog and issue context with actor-visible fields.
entries.statement: Do not support unbounded Jira reads such as broad issue export or unrestricted project-wide search results.

entries.rationale: Comments, subtasks, stories, incident bugs, assignee changes, sprint moves, workflow transitions, issue links, and release notes are described as preview-first or approval-gated.
entries.statement: Do not let write-adjacent Jira actions execute immediately without stopping at preview or explicit approval.

entries.rationale: The governance posture explicitly requires clarification instead of guessing.
entries.statement: Do not guess missing required Jira inputs or infer action details when clarification is needed.

entries.rationale: Reads are intended to be bounded and actor-visible rather than globally expanded.
entries.statement: Do not return data outside the requesting actor's visible Jira scope.

data.policy_summary: Expose governed Jira business capabilities instead of raw REST or MCP tools. Allow bounded read-only issue and backlog context. Require write-adjacent Jira actions to stop at preview or explicit approval, return clarification when required inputs are missing, and record audit evidence for governed actions.

rules.governed_outcome: Allow bounded, reviewable jira context access behavior for jira requester when the request stays within actor-visible scope; clarify missing critical context, deny raw export or unsupported mutation, and stop for approval before side effects.
rules.notes: Studio-derived review candidate because the assistant produced a policy summary but no concrete actor-by-business-area rules. Confirm or edit before locking Product Design.

rules.governed_outcome: Allow bounded, reviewable governed jira change requests behavior for jira requester when the request stays within actor-visible scope; clarify missing critical context, deny raw export or unsupported mutation, and stop for approval before side effects.
rules.notes: Studio-derived review candidate because the assistant produced a policy summary but no concrete actor-by-business-area rules. Confirm or edit before locking Product Design.

rules.governed_outcome: Allow bounded, reviewable approval and audit controls behavior for jira requester when the request stays within actor-visible scope; clarify missing critical context, deny raw export or unsupported mutation, and stop for approval before side effects.
rules.notes: Studio-derived review candidate because the assistant produced a policy summary but no concrete actor-by-business-area rules. Confirm or edit before locking Product Design.

rules.governed_outcome: Allow bounded, reviewable jira context access behavior for jira approver when the request stays within actor-visible scope; clarify missing critical context, deny raw export or unsupported mutation, and stop for approval before side effects.
rules.notes: Studio-derived review candidate because the assistant produced a policy summary but no concrete actor-by-business-area rules. Confirm or edit before locking Product Design.

rules.governed_outcome: Allow bounded, reviewable governed jira change requests behavior for jira approver when the request stays within actor-visible scope; clarify missing critical context, deny raw export or unsupported mutation, and stop for approval before side effects.
rules.notes: Studio-derived review candidate because the assistant produced a policy summary but no concrete actor-by-business-area rules. Confirm or edit before locking Product Design.

rules.governed_outcome: Allow bounded, reviewable approval and audit controls behavior for jira approver when the request stays within actor-visible scope; clarify missing critical context, deny raw export or unsupported mutation, and stop for approval before side effects.
rules.notes: Studio-derived review candidate because the assistant produced a policy summary but no concrete actor-by-business-area rules. Confirm or edit before locking Product Design.

data.approval_posture_summary: Read-only Jira context can proceed within bounded visibility limits. Write-adjacent actions must stop at preview or approval before execution, with policy checks and audit evidence recorded for governed requests.
business_goals: Give agents bounded, actor-visible Jira issue and backlog context for read-only work.
Replace raw Jira tool access with governed business capabilities.
Ensure write-adjacent Jira actions stop at preview or explicit approval before execution.
Record audit evidence for governed write-adjacent requests and approvals.
Trigger clarification when required inputs are missing instead of guessing.
data.business_problem: Agents need Jira support that is safe and usable, but direct Jira APIs and MCP-backed operations expose raw tool surfaces, unbounded access patterns, and write paths that can bypass review, visibility limits, and policy controls.
data.governed_behavior_summary: The product should return only bounded, actor-visible Jira context for reads; never expose raw Jira tools directly; prepare previews for write-adjacent actions; require explicit approval when policy requires execution; and ask clarifying questions when required inputs are missing.
data.product_purpose: Provide a governed Jira fronting experience that lets agents access bounded Jira context and prepare or request Jira actions without exposing raw Jira APIs or MCP tools.

entries.evidence: PM review of representative read flows shows users receive actor-visible issue or backlog context through governed capability surfaces, and no raw tool or unbounded query path is presented.
entries.statement: Agents can access Jira through bounded business capabilities for issue and backlog context without exposure to raw REST, MCP tools, or unbounded JQL-style querying.

entries.evidence: Verification of issue-context and backlog-context scenarios shows returned data is scoped, actor-visible, and bounded to allowed project or issue context.
entries.statement: Read-only Jira access returns bounded, actor-visible context rather than unrestricted issue export or hidden field access.

entries.evidence: PM review across representative actions such as comments, subtasks, stories, incident bugs, assignee changes, sprint moves, workflow transitions, issue links, and release notes shows the product produces a preview or approval request rather than silently executing changes.
entries.statement: Write-adjacent Jira actions stop at preview or explicit approval boundaries before any mutation is carried out.

entries.evidence: Negative-path review shows incomplete or policy-sensitive requests trigger visible clarification or denial behavior with no inferred mutation-ready output.
entries.statement: When required inputs are missing or policy checks block progress, the product asks for clarification or stops explicitly instead of guessing.

entries.evidence: PM or compliance review can inspect audit artifacts for representative governed actions and confirm they capture the request, preview or approval boundary, and resulting decision state.
entries.statement: Approval-gated and preview-based Jira flows produce reviewable audit evidence for what was requested, what was shown, and why execution did or did not proceed.

next_steps: Review and accept or edit the proposed two-service public shape.
Confirm final ownership for jira.release_notes.prepare and jira.subtask.prepare.
If accepted, carry the confirmed service boundaries into Developer Design capability contracts and governance bindings.
After ownership is locked, map each public capability to approval, clarification, scope, and audit behavior without exposing internal execution seams.

items.body: Use the locked product topology as the developer-facing public service shape. Keep one service focused on bounded Jira context reads and one service focused on governed previews and request flows for write-adjacent actions. Do not expose internal adapter, execution, or raw-tool seams as public services.
items.rationale: The baseline explicitly provides a two-service public shape and the topology preference requires preserving source-defined boundaries at exactly two services.

coordination.description: Governed action flows may depend on bounded issue or backlog context gathered under actor-visible scope before preparing previews or approval-ready requests.

shape.name: Jira Governed Fronting Public Surface
notes: Public governed capability surface only.
Internal Jira API, MCP, adapter, and execution details are intentionally excluded from Product Service Design.
Read capabilities return bounded actor-visible context.
Write-adjacent capabilities prepare previews or requests and stop at approval boundaries when policy requires.
Clarification on missing required inputs is part of the governed contract.

services.name: Jira Context Service
responsibilities: Provide bounded backlog and issue context to browser users and agents
Enforce actor-visible scope on projects, issues, comments, and history
Prevent exposure of raw JQL, raw Jira REST semantics, MCP tools, or unbounded export behavior
Return clarification when required read inputs are missing or ambiguous
services.role: public governed read surface for bounded Jira context

services.name: Jira Governed Action Service
responsibilities: Prepare governed previews for Jira write-adjacent actions without directly mutating Jira
Handle approval-gated request flows and explicit stop conditions
Ask for clarification when required action inputs, target scope, or policy context are missing
Record audit evidence for previews, approval-required requests, and policy-driven stops
Apply actor-visible scope and policy checks before any mutation boundary is crossed
Produce governed draft artifacts such as release notes and customer-safe comments
services.role: public governed preview and request surface for Jira write-adjacent actions and governed drafting

items.title: Preserve the locked two-service public governed surface

items.body: Treat the source-declared capability IDs as candidate canonical public IDs and assign each to exactly one owning public service. Current split keeps bounded read context in Jira Context Service and all governed preview/request flows in Jira Governed Action Service, with jira.release_notes.prepare flagged for review because it is read-only in developer evidence but behaves like a governed draft artifact in product intent.
items.rationale: The source provides explicit capability IDs and a preserved two-service topology, but one capability has mixed read-vs-governed-draft signals.
items.title: Confirm canonical public capability ownership

items.body: Developer Design should preserve the public governed capability surface only: bounded issue context, backlog context, governed previews, and approval-ready requests. Do not publish adapter or execution capabilities such as jira.adapter.* or backend operation names as public capabilities, and do not expose raw JQL, raw REST endpoints, or unrestricted Jira tool semantics through the browser or agent surface.
items.rationale: The locked baseline repeatedly states that the product must front Jira through governed business capabilities rather than raw tools.
items.title: Keep the public surface business-oriented and non-raw

items.body: For governed action capabilities, define preview, approval-required, denied, and clarification-needed outcomes as explicit contract states rather than hidden implementation details. For read capabilities, define bounded-result, denied, and clarification-needed outcomes. Ensure both services preserve lineage and durable searchable audit evidence for previews, approvals, and policy-driven stops.
items.rationale: Approval gating, clarification on missing inputs, actor-visible scope enforcement, and audit evidence are core locked requirements across scenarios.
items.title: Make stop states first-class in service contracts

questions_for_user: Confirm that the public service topology should remain exactly two services: Jira Context Service and Jira Governed Action Service.

Confirm the candidate public capability ownership split: Jira Context Service owns jira.backlog.search_context and jira.issue.get_context; Jira Governed Action Service owns jira.incident_bug.prepare, jira.story.prepare, jira.subtask.prepare, jira.customer_escalation.comment.prepare, jira.workflow_transition.request, jira.sprint_move.request, jira.assignee_change.request, jira.issue_link.request, and jira.release_notes.prepare.

Should jira.release_notes.prepare remain with Jira Governed Action Service because it produces a governed draft artifact, or move to Jira Context Service because it is read-only in developer evidence?
Confirm whether jira.subtask.prepare should stay in scope for the public surface even though it appears in developer evidence but is not called out in the locked scenario list.
Do you want canonical service IDs added now, or should service names remain the primary confirmed boundary until PM/dev review?
envelope.summary: Candidate developer-facing service design blocks derived from the locked product baseline. The proposal preserves the source-defined two-service public governed surface and excludes internal adapter or backend execution capabilities from the public design.
envelope.title: Service Design Proposal: Jira Governed Fronting Gate Clean
watchouts: This is a review proposal only and should be explicitly accepted before any persistence.

The project is a governed fronting public HTTP service, so the public service design should expose only governed business capabilities, not raw Jira REST, MCP, adapter, backend, or execution surfaces.
A capability ID should have exactly one owning public service; do not duplicate the same capability across both services.

The locked baseline prefers production_single_service at runtime scale, but the locked product service shape explicitly preserves a two-service public capability topology; treat this as a logical public boundary unless PM intentionally changes it.
jira.release_notes.prepare is ambiguous because product scenarios frame it as governed artifact preparation while developer evidence marks it read-only; ownership should be confirmed.
source-declared capability IDs appear canonical in the evidence and should be preserved exactly if accepted.

next_steps: Review the extracted input contracts in Capability Formalization.
Save only after the developer-owned interface evidence matches the intended runtime surface.

next_steps: Confirm runtime actor classes and approval authority model.
Decide whether policy bindings should be capability-level only or also include per-input conditions such as project, sprint, and issue references.
Define explicit restricted_vs_denied outcome semantics for out-of-scope Jira reads.
Add concrete audit evidence requirements tied to lineage fields: task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id.
Review each candidate block for acceptance, edits, or rejection before persistence.

items.body: For jira.issue.get_context and jira.backlog.search_context, allow runtime execution only when the requested issue or project is within actor-visible Jira scope. Return bounded context only, limited to actor-visible fields, comments, history, and backlog results. If scope is missing, unresolved, or ambiguous, stop with clarification_required. If policy determines the target is out of scope, do not reveal out-of-scope Jira data and return the configured restricted or denied outcome explicitly.
items.rationale: The baseline explicitly requires bounded read-only Jira context, actor-visible scope enforcement on every governed action, clarification on missing scope, and no raw JQL or unrestricted retrieval.
items.title: Bind read capabilities to actor-visible Jira scope only

items.body: Bind the public runtime surface so callers can invoke only governed Jira business capabilities, not raw Jira REST endpoints, raw MCP tools, or raw JQL/query semantics. Any backend use of Jira operations must remain internal and must not appear as callable runtime actions or user-visible tool affordances in browser or agent flows.
items.rationale: The product baseline and scenarios repeatedly require governed fronting rather than raw tools, especially for browser-verifiable flows.
items.title: Prohibit raw Jira tool and query exposure on the public runtime surface

items.body: For jira.incident_bug.prepare, jira.story.prepare, jira.subtask.prepare, jira.customer_escalation.comment.prepare, jira.workflow_transition.request, jira.sprint_move.request, jira.assignee_change.request, and jira.issue_link.request, bind runtime behavior to stop before direct Jira mutation. The runtime result must be a preview artifact or approval-ready request. No silent execution is allowed from the public governed surface.
items.rationale: The locked baseline states that write-adjacent Jira actions must stop at preview or approval and must not mutate Jira directly from the governed surface.
items.title: Force preview-or-request posture for all write-adjacent Jira capabilities

items.body: Bind all governed Jira capabilities so missing, ambiguous, or unresolved required inputs produce clarification_required rather than guessed defaults. This includes issue identifiers, project scope, bounded issue sets, assignee references, target workflow status, target sprint, relationship type, and other required business inputs. Clarification prompts should name the missing business input needed to continue.
items.rationale: Clarification is part of the governed contract in the baseline and is reinforced by the scenario set and developer input evidence.
items.title: Treat missing or ambiguous required inputs as clarification_required

items.body: For write-adjacent request capabilities, bind runtime policy so approval-required cases return an explicit approval boundary state with a reviewable request payload, not a partial execution state. The runtime contract should distinguish at least preview_prepared, approval_required, denied, restricted, and clarification_required outcomes.
items.rationale: The baseline requires approval stops where policy says so, and the scenarios call for explicit stop conditions and reviewable requests.
items.title: Make approval-required outcomes first-class runtime states

items.body: For jira.issue_link.request, require both issue references and a declared relationship type, then evaluate cross-project policy before any request can advance beyond preview or approval-ready form. If cross-project policy is blocked or unclear, stop explicitly and record the disposition. Do not infer allowed project relationships from issue visibility alone.
items.rationale: Cross-project checks are explicit in the scenario, but the exact allow and deny rules are not defined in the baseline.
items.title: Apply explicit cross-project policy checks before issue link requests advance

items.body: For jira.sprint_move.request, bind runtime evaluation to actor-visible board scope and resolvable sprint scope before preparing the request. Support multi-issue requests only when all requested issues and the target sprint are within allowed visible scope. Mixed-scope requests should stop explicitly rather than partially proceeding.
items.rationale: The sprint move scenario explicitly requires actor-visible board scope, approval posture, and auditable request handling.
items.title: Constrain sprint move requests to actor-visible board and sprint scope

items.body: For jira.assignee_change.request, bind runtime policy so the requested assignee must be resolved against Jira identity before a preview or approval-ready request is returned. Ambiguous or unresolved assignee references must trigger clarification_required. The preview should include both the requested assignee reference and the resolved Jira identity when resolution succeeds.
items.rationale: The assignee change scenario and developer evidence explicitly require backend user resolution, preview, approval, and audit.
items.title: Require backend assignee resolution before assignee change preview

items.body: For jira.release_notes.prepare, bind runtime behavior to require explicit bounded project and release scope plus a bounded issue selection input. The runtime result is a draft artifact only. It must not publish release notes, mutate Jira, or broaden into unbounded issue export. If the issue set is too broad or unspecified, return clarification_required.
items.rationale: The release notes scenario and baseline require bounded aggregation, non-publishing behavior, and clarification when scope is too broad.
items.title: Keep release note preparation bounded, draft-only, and non-publishing

items.body: Bind runtime policy so governed Jira outcomes emit durable, searchable audit evidence for reads, previews, approval-required requests, denials, restrictions, and clarification stops. Evidence should capture what capability was requested, the bounded Jira target scope, the policy result, the preview or returned bounded result, and why execution did or did not proceed. Include lineage fields required by the baseline: task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id when present.
items.rationale: Durable searchable audit, explicit lineage, and evidence for previews and policy-driven stops are direct baseline requirements.
items.title: Record audit evidence for governed reads, previews, approvals, and stops

questions_for_user: Which concrete runtime actor classes should be bound now, beyond the generic 'authorized user' in the baseline?
For approval-required Jira actions, who is the approval authority at runtime: the requesting user, a separate reviewer, or an external policy system?
Should release note preparation be treated as read-only draft generation with no approval, or should some audiences require approval before draft return?
Do cross-project issue links have a default deny posture unless an explicit allowlist exists?
Should browser users and agent callers share the same runtime policy bindings, or do they need separate bindings for the same Jira capabilities?
envelope.summary: Candidate runtime policy binding blocks derived from the locked Jira baseline, scenario set, and developer evidence. Focus is on actor-visible scope, preview-only write posture, clarification stops, approval boundaries, and audit evidence.
envelope.title: Runtime Policy Binding Proposal: Jira Governed Fronting Gate Clean
watchouts: The baseline requires signed trust mode but does not define concrete signer or verifier bindings.
Approval is required in principle for write-adjacent actions, but the runtime approver identity and handoff path are not specified.
Actor-visible scope is central, but project, board, sprint, and issue visibility sources are not yet formalized.
Read capabilities must avoid raw Jira REST, raw MCP tools, and raw JQL exposure even if backend seams use them internally.
Clarification-required and denied outcomes must stay distinct; missing inputs should not collapse into access denial.
Audit evidence is required for previews, approval stops, and policy-driven stops, but minimum evidence fields are not fully enumerated.

next_steps: Confirm the downstream Jira integration target and auth posture.
Decide whether first release supports preview-only or full approval-to-execution handoff for write-adjacent requests.
Choose whether to keep separate read and action backend bindings or collapse to one service binding for the initial deployment.
Define the policy evaluation source for actor-visible scope, restricted-vs-denied outcomes, and approval checks.
Define the audit sink and lineage fields that every binding must emit.

items.body: Create a backend binding layer that maps each governed capability to explicit HTTP integration endpoints rather than exposing raw Jira operations. Read bindings should cover bounded backlog context, single-issue context, and release-notes preparation. Write-adjacent bindings should target preview or request endpoints for story, incident bug, subtask, customer-safe comment, workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue link flows. All calls should use signed HTTP and preserve purpose binding, scoped authority, delegation tokens, and lineage fields.
items.rationale: The baseline requires public HTTP transport, signed trust mode, governed capability entry points, and no raw Jira tool exposure. Integration evidence already suggests HTTP endpoint shapes for these governed flows.
items.title: Bind governed public capabilities to signed HTTP Jira integration endpoints

items.body: Define two backend binding groups: one for bounded read-only Jira context and one for write-adjacent preview/request flows. The read group should enforce actor-visible scope and bounded result shaping before returning issue or backlog context. The action group should prepare previews or approval-ready requests, never silently mutate Jira, and make stop states explicit when approval or clarification is required.
items.rationale: The locked service design separates read and governed action surfaces, and the requirements distinguish bounded read access from approval-gated write-adjacent behavior.
items.title: Separate bounded read bindings from approval-gated action bindings

items.body: For assignee change, sprint move, workflow transition, issue link, comment drafting, story drafting, subtask drafting, and incident bug drafting, bind the backend so the first runtime step always produces a preview or approval-ready request artifact. Any eventual Jira mutation binding should sit behind an explicit approval boundary and should not be reachable from the public capability surface without approved grant context.
items.rationale: The baseline explicitly says write-adjacent actions must stop at preview or approval and must not execute silently. Developer evidence also models these as approval-gated operations.
items.title: Bind write-adjacent capabilities through preview-first execution gates

items.body: Add a policy binding stage ahead of Jira integration calls. For reads, evaluate actor-visible project, issue, comment, history, board, sprint, and cross-project visibility before data retrieval or response shaping. For write-adjacent requests, evaluate approval policy, restricted-vs-denied outcomes, and cross-project link constraints before producing a preview or allowing any post-approval execution path.
items.rationale: The baseline requires actor-visible scope enforcement, preflight discovery, restricted-vs-denied handling, and policy checks on every governed action, but no concrete backend binding for that evaluation is yet defined.
items.title: Bind every capability to explicit scope and policy evaluation before backend execution

items.body: Require every backend binding to emit durable, searchable audit records for reads, previews, approval-required stops, denials, clarifications, and any later approved execution. Audit payloads should include requested capability, bounded target scope, applied policy result, preview artifact reference when produced, and lineage fields for task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id.
items.rationale: Audit durability, searchability, and lineage propagation are explicit locked requirements and are central to governed stop conditions and browser-verifiable review flows.
items.title: Bind all governed flows to durable searchable audit with lineage propagation

items.body: Implement backend resolution bindings for project keys, issue keys, assignee references, workflow statuses, sprint references, release references, components, and link types before constructing Jira-facing requests. Missing, ambiguous, or unresolved references should return clarification or denial according to the reviewed input contracts instead of guessing defaults.
items.rationale: Developer evidence formalizes backend-resolved inputs across most capabilities, and the baseline requires clarification when required inputs or target scope are missing or ambiguous.
items.title: Bind entity resolution through backend catalogs before Jira-facing requests

items.body: Define an internal backend adapter seam that encapsulates Jira-specific reads, assignee resolution, and post-approval execution operations. The public governed capability layer should call this seam indirectly so the implementation can swap native Jira REST and Atlassian MCP without changing the external governed contract or browser-visible behavior.
items.rationale: Source evidence explicitly allows either native API or MCP behind the same governed contract, and developer evidence already distinguishes public governed capabilities from internal adapter operations.
items.title: Keep an internal adapter seam for Jira-specific operations behind the governed contract

items.body: Specify the initial Jira environment target and the secret reference contract for base URL and credentials or signing material. Bindings should reference secret handles only, never embedded tokens, and should support deployment-time configuration for Jira base URL and optional MCP endpoint without changing capability contracts.
items.rationale: Integration evidence names connection points and secret posture, but the actual environment target and auth reference names are still unspecified and should be explicit before generation.
items.title: Make Jira environment target and secret references explicit in backend bindings

questions_for_user: Should the generated backend bind to native Jira REST, Atlassian MCP, or support both behind one governed contract?
What environment label should bindings target first: production, staging, or sandbox?
Should approval-gated execution endpoints remain enabled in the first backend pass, or should first release stop at preview-only for all write-adjacent flows?
What secret reference names or auth mechanism should the Jira binding expect for signed HTTP calls?
Do you want separate backend bindings for bounded read flows versus approval-gated action flows, or one shared Jira integration binding?
envelope.summary: Candidate backend-binding blocks derived from the locked Jira governed fronting baseline and developer evidence. Focus is on explicit Jira integration seams, approval-gated execution paths, and runtime governance wiring for review before persistence.
envelope.title: Backend Binding Proposal: Jira Governed Fronting Gate Clean
watchouts: The locked baseline says the product must not expose raw Jira REST, raw MCP tools, or raw JQL directly; backend bindings must stay behind governed capability endpoints.
Service shape notes conflict with requirements on scale: requirements prefer production_single_service, while current service design is multi_service.
Developer evidence includes internal adapter operations, but the locked canonical public capability inventory must remain limited to the 11 listed governed capabilities.

Several write-adjacent capabilities reference eventual Jira mutation operations in backend evidence; first-release browser verification may still require preview-only behavior unless approval execution is explicitly enabled.
Cross-project policy checks, actor-visible scope evaluation, and approval policy sources are required by baseline but not yet bound to a concrete policy engine or rule source.
Audit is required to be durable and searchable; backend bindings should not assume transient logs are sufficient.
Signed trust mode is required; bindings should not default to unsigned internal calls even if the first deployment is single-service HTTP.

next_steps: Review and accept, edit, or reject the candidate verification blocks individually.
Decide whether internal adapter verification belongs in the same artifact or a separate developer evidence review.
After acceptance, translate approved blocks into explicit verification expectations and coverage criteria for the release.

items.body: Verification should prove that bounded read capabilities return only actor-visible Jira context and do not broaden into raw or unbounded access. For single-issue inspection, evidence should show one issue at a time with bounded fields, comments, and optional history only when requested. For backlog context, evidence should show project-bounded, actor-visible results and confirm that missing project scope triggers clarification instead of guessed defaults. Verification should also confirm that raw Jira REST semantics, raw MCP tools, and raw JQL are not exposed through the browser UI or public capability contract.
items.rationale: The baseline explicitly requires bounded read-only Jira context, actor-visible scope enforcement, clarification on missing inputs, and no raw tool exposure.
items.title: Verify bounded read behavior on the public Jira context surface

items.body: Verification should prove that write-adjacent capabilities produce a governed preview or request artifact and do not directly mutate Jira before approval conditions are satisfied. This should cover story preparation, incident bug preparation, customer-safe comment drafting, release-note drafting where applicable, assignee change requests, sprint move requests, workflow transition requests, and issue link requests. Evidence should show preview content or approval-ready request state, plus explicit proof that no Jira mutation occurred during the verification step.
items.rationale: The locked requirements state that comments, stories, bugs, assignee changes, sprint moves, workflow transitions, issue links, and release notes must stop at preview or approval rather than silently execute.
items.title: Verify preview-only posture for write-adjacent Jira capabilities

items.body: Verification should prove that the governed surface asks targeted clarification questions whenever required scope, target identifiers, relationship type, assignee identity, release scope, or other mandatory business inputs are missing, ambiguous, or unresolved. Evidence should show that the system does not guess defaults for unsafe values and that clarification prompts identify the specific missing input needed to continue. Coverage should include at least one read flow and multiple write-adjacent flows with different ambiguity types, such as missing issue key, broad release issue set, ambiguous assignee reference, and unresolved cross-project link details.
items.rationale: Clarification on missing required inputs is a locked requirement and is reinforced by the developer input contracts across capabilities.
items.title: Verify clarification behavior when required business inputs are missing or ambiguous

items.body: Verification should prove that governed action flows evaluate scope and approval policy before any mutation boundary is crossed, and that approval-required cases stop explicitly in a reviewable state. Evidence should show the difference between allowed preview generation, approval-required stop, and denied or restricted outcomes without leaking out-of-scope Jira data. For workflow transitions, sprint moves, assignee changes, and issue linking, verification should confirm that policy evaluation is visible in the outcome and that blocked or unclear cases do not proceed silently.
items.rationale: The baseline requires actor-visible scope and approval policy enforcement on every governed action, with explicit stops where approval or policy blocks apply.
items.title: Verify approval boundaries and explicit policy-driven stop states

items.body: Verification should prove that previews, approval-required requests, and policy-driven stop conditions generate durable and searchable audit evidence. Evidence should capture what was requested, what Jira scope and policy were applied, what preview or stop outcome was produced, and why execution did or did not proceed. Verification should also confirm presence of lineage fields required by the baseline, including task identifier, invocation identifier, client reference identifier, and parent invocation identifier where applicable.
items.rationale: Audit durability, searchability, and lineage fields are explicit locked requirements and are central to governed fronting credibility.
items.title: Verify durable, searchable audit evidence and lineage for governed outcomes

items.body: Verification should include a browser-verifiable scenario pack that demonstrates the governed fronting behavior end to end. At minimum, the pack should show bounded issue inspection, bounded backlog context retrieval, a write-adjacent preview flow, an approval-required stop flow, a clarification flow for missing inputs, and visible absence of raw Jira tools in the UI. The expectation is not only backend correctness but also clean browser evidence that the product presents Jira as business-oriented governed capabilities rather than raw operational tooling.
items.rationale: The first-release requirement explicitly calls for clean browser UI verification of governed Jira fronting and lists the behaviors that must be demonstrated.
items.title: Verify first-release browser UI evidence for governed fronting end to end

items.body: Verification should map evidence to the locked public capability inventory and the baseline scenarios so coverage is explicit rather than ad hoc. Expectations should ensure both read capabilities are covered, and that each write-adjacent capability family has at least one representative verification path showing preview, clarification, approval stop, or policy evaluation as appropriate. Scenario coverage should especially include release notes from bounded issues, customer-safe comment drafting without posting, assignee change with identity resolution, sprint move with actor-visible board scope, workflow transition with approval stop, issue linking with cross-project policy checks, story preview with acceptance criteria, incident bug preview, single-issue context inspection, and bounded backlog context discovery.
items.rationale: The baseline provides a concrete scenario set and canonical capability inventory, so verification expectations should preserve that coverage explicitly.
items.title: Verify coverage against the locked capability inventory and scenario set

questions_for_user: Should verification expectations cover only the public governed capability surface, or also internal adapter seams as supporting evidence?
Do you want browser UI verification called out as a required evidence source for every first-release capability, or only for representative end-to-end flows?
Should approval-required capabilities be verified only up to preview and approval-stop states in this release, or should post-approval execution evidence also be expected where internal seams exist?
envelope.summary: Candidate developer-facing verification expectation blocks derived from the locked Jira baseline, scenario set, and developer evidence. Focus is on proving bounded read behavior, preview-only write-adjacent behavior, approval stops, clarification handling, audit evidence, and clean browser-verifiable governed fronting.
envelope.title: Verification Expectations Proposal: Jira Governed Fronting Gate Clean
watchouts: The locked baseline says the first release is browser-verifiable and must not expose raw Jira REST, raw MCP tools, or raw JQL through the UI.
Several write-adjacent capabilities are prepare/request flows; verification must prove non-mutation before approval, not just happy-path preview rendering.

Cross-project issue linking has medium-confidence policy detail in the source, so verification should focus on explicit stop behavior and auditable policy evaluation rather than assuming exact allow rules.
Developer evidence includes internal adapter operations, but the public contract is the governed surface; internal checks should support, not replace, public-surface verification.
Audit expectations should verify durable, searchable evidence for previews, approvals, and policy-driven stops, including lineage fields.

bundle.summary: Review this locked-baseline-derived Developer Design bundle and save accepted proposal sections into assistant review artifacts.
bundle.title: Developer Design Draft: Jira Governed Fronting Gate Clean

accepted_payload.body: Use the locked product topology as the developer-facing public service shape. Keep one service focused on bounded Jira context reads and one service focused on governed previews and request flows for write-adjacent actions. Do not expose internal adapter, execution, or raw-tool seams as public services.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline explicitly provides a two-service public shape and the topology preference requires preserving source-defined boundaries at exactly two services.

coordination.description: Governed action flows may depend on bounded issue or backlog context gathered under actor-visible scope before preparing previews or approval-ready requests.

accepted_payload.title: Preserve the locked two-service public governed surface

accepted_payload.body: Treat the source-declared capability IDs as candidate canonical public IDs and assign each to exactly one owning public service. Current split keeps bounded read context in Jira Context Service and all governed preview/request flows in Jira Governed Action Service, with jira.release_notes.prepare flagged for review because it is read-only in developer evidence but behaves like a governed draft artifact in product intent.
accepted_payload.rationale: The source provides explicit capability IDs and a preserved two-service topology, but one capability has mixed read-vs-governed-draft signals.
accepted_payload.title: Confirm canonical public capability ownership

accepted_payload.body: Developer Design should preserve the public governed capability surface only: bounded issue context, backlog context, governed previews, and approval-ready requests. Do not publish adapter or execution capabilities such as jira.adapter.* or backend operation names as public capabilities, and do not expose raw JQL, raw REST endpoints, or unrestricted Jira tool semantics through the browser or agent surface.
accepted_payload.rationale: The locked baseline repeatedly states that the product must front Jira through governed business capabilities rather than raw tools.
accepted_payload.title: Keep the public surface business-oriented and non-raw

accepted_payload.body: For governed action capabilities, define preview, approval-required, denied, and clarification-needed outcomes as explicit contract states rather than hidden implementation details. For read capabilities, define bounded-result, denied, and clarification-needed outcomes. Ensure both services preserve lineage and durable searchable audit evidence for previews, approvals, and policy-driven stops.
accepted_payload.rationale: Approval gating, clarification on missing inputs, actor-visible scope enforcement, and audit evidence are core locked requirements across scenarios.
accepted_payload.title: Make stop states first-class in service contracts
