items.title: Canonical capability formalizations

accepted_payload.body: For jira.issue.get_context and jira.backlog.search_context, allow runtime execution only when the requested issue or project is within actor-visible Jira scope. Return bounded context only, limited to actor-visible fields, comments, history, and backlog results. If scope is missing, unresolved, or ambiguous, stop with clarification_required. If policy determines the target is out of scope, do not reveal out-of-scope Jira data and return the configured restricted or denied outcome explicitly.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline explicitly requires bounded read-only Jira context, actor-visible scope enforcement on every governed action, clarification on missing scope, and no raw JQL or unrestricted retrieval.
accepted_payload.title: Bind read capabilities to actor-visible Jira scope only

accepted_payload.body: Bind the public runtime surface so callers can invoke only governed Jira business capabilities, not raw Jira REST endpoints, raw MCP tools, or raw JQL/query semantics. Any backend use of Jira operations must remain internal and must not appear as callable runtime actions or user-visible tool affordances in browser or agent flows.
accepted_payload.rationale: The product baseline and scenarios repeatedly require governed fronting rather than raw tools, especially for browser-verifiable flows.
accepted_payload.title: Prohibit raw Jira tool and query exposure on the public runtime surface

accepted_payload.body: For jira.incident_bug.prepare, jira.story.prepare, jira.subtask.prepare, jira.customer_escalation.comment.prepare, jira.workflow_transition.request, jira.sprint_move.request, jira.assignee_change.request, and jira.issue_link.request, bind runtime behavior to stop before direct Jira mutation. The runtime result must be a preview artifact or approval-ready request. No silent execution is allowed from the public governed surface.
accepted_payload.rationale: The locked baseline states that write-adjacent Jira actions must stop at preview or approval and must not mutate Jira directly from the governed surface.
accepted_payload.title: Force preview-or-request posture for all write-adjacent Jira capabilities

accepted_payload.body: Bind all governed Jira capabilities so missing, ambiguous, or unresolved required inputs produce clarification_required rather than guessed defaults. This includes issue identifiers, project scope, bounded issue sets, assignee references, target workflow status, target sprint, relationship type, and other required business inputs. Clarification prompts should name the missing business input needed to continue.
accepted_payload.rationale: Clarification is part of the governed contract in the baseline and is reinforced by the scenario set and developer input evidence.
accepted_payload.title: Treat missing or ambiguous required inputs as clarification_required

accepted_payload.body: For write-adjacent request capabilities, bind runtime policy so approval-required cases return an explicit approval boundary state with a reviewable request payload, not a partial execution state. The runtime contract should distinguish at least preview_prepared, approval_required, denied, restricted, and clarification_required outcomes.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline requires approval stops where policy says so, and the scenarios call for explicit stop conditions and reviewable requests.
accepted_payload.title: Make approval-required outcomes first-class runtime states

accepted_payload.body: For jira.issue_link.request, require both issue references and a declared relationship type, then evaluate cross-project policy before any request can advance beyond preview or approval-ready form. If cross-project policy is blocked or unclear, stop explicitly and record the disposition. Do not infer allowed project relationships from issue visibility alone.
accepted_payload.rationale: Cross-project checks are explicit in the scenario, but the exact allow and deny rules are not defined in the baseline.
accepted_payload.title: Apply explicit cross-project policy checks before issue link requests advance

accepted_payload.body: For jira.sprint_move.request, bind runtime evaluation to actor-visible board scope and resolvable sprint scope before preparing the request. Support multi-issue requests only when all requested issues and the target sprint are within allowed visible scope. Mixed-scope requests should stop explicitly rather than partially proceeding.
accepted_payload.rationale: The sprint move scenario explicitly requires actor-visible board scope, approval posture, and auditable request handling.
accepted_payload.title: Constrain sprint move requests to actor-visible board and sprint scope

accepted_payload.body: For jira.assignee_change.request, bind runtime policy so the requested assignee must be resolved against Jira identity before a preview or approval-ready request is returned. Ambiguous or unresolved assignee references must trigger clarification_required. The preview should include both the requested assignee reference and the resolved Jira identity when resolution succeeds.
accepted_payload.rationale: The assignee change scenario and developer evidence explicitly require backend user resolution, preview, approval, and audit.
accepted_payload.title: Require backend assignee resolution before assignee change preview

accepted_payload.body: For jira.release_notes.prepare, bind runtime behavior to require explicit bounded project and release scope plus a bounded issue selection input. The runtime result is a draft artifact only. It must not publish release notes, mutate Jira, or broaden into unbounded issue export. If the issue set is too broad or unspecified, return clarification_required.
accepted_payload.rationale: The release notes scenario and baseline require bounded aggregation, non-publishing behavior, and clarification when scope is too broad.
accepted_payload.title: Keep release note preparation bounded, draft-only, and non-publishing

accepted_payload.body: Bind runtime policy so governed Jira outcomes emit durable, searchable audit evidence for reads, previews, approval-required requests, denials, restrictions, and clarification stops. Evidence should capture what capability was requested, the bounded Jira target scope, the policy result, the preview or returned bounded result, and why execution did or did not proceed. Include lineage fields required by the baseline: task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id when present.
accepted_payload.rationale: Durable searchable audit, explicit lineage, and evidence for previews and policy-driven stops are direct baseline requirements.
accepted_payload.title: Record audit evidence for governed reads, previews, approvals, and stops

pm_artifacts.title: Runtime Policy Binding Proposal: Jira Governed Fronting Gate Clean

accepted_payload.body: Create a backend binding layer that maps each governed capability to explicit HTTP integration endpoints rather than exposing raw Jira operations. Read bindings should cover bounded backlog context, single-issue context, and release-notes preparation. Write-adjacent bindings should target preview or request endpoints for story, incident bug, subtask, customer-safe comment, workflow transition, sprint move, assignee change, and issue link flows. All calls should use signed HTTP and preserve purpose binding, scoped authority, delegation tokens, and lineage fields.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline requires public HTTP transport, signed trust mode, governed capability entry points, and no raw Jira tool exposure. Integration evidence already suggests HTTP endpoint shapes for these governed flows.
accepted_payload.title: Bind governed public capabilities to signed HTTP Jira integration endpoints

accepted_payload.body: Define two backend binding groups: one for bounded read-only Jira context and one for write-adjacent preview/request flows. The read group should enforce actor-visible scope and bounded result shaping before returning issue or backlog context. The action group should prepare previews or approval-ready requests, never silently mutate Jira, and make stop states explicit when approval or clarification is required.
accepted_payload.rationale: The locked service design separates read and governed action surfaces, and the requirements distinguish bounded read access from approval-gated write-adjacent behavior.
accepted_payload.title: Separate bounded read bindings from approval-gated action bindings

accepted_payload.body: For assignee change, sprint move, workflow transition, issue link, comment drafting, story drafting, subtask drafting, and incident bug drafting, bind the backend so the first runtime step always produces a preview or approval-ready request artifact. Any eventual Jira mutation binding should sit behind an explicit approval boundary and should not be reachable from the public capability surface without approved grant context.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline explicitly says write-adjacent actions must stop at preview or approval and must not execute silently. Developer evidence also models these as approval-gated operations.
accepted_payload.title: Bind write-adjacent capabilities through preview-first execution gates

accepted_payload.body: Add a policy binding stage ahead of Jira integration calls. For reads, evaluate actor-visible project, issue, comment, history, board, sprint, and cross-project visibility before data retrieval or response shaping. For write-adjacent requests, evaluate approval policy, restricted-vs-denied outcomes, and cross-project link constraints before producing a preview or allowing any post-approval execution path.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline requires actor-visible scope enforcement, preflight discovery, restricted-vs-denied handling, and policy checks on every governed action, but no concrete backend binding for that evaluation is yet defined.
accepted_payload.title: Bind every capability to explicit scope and policy evaluation before backend execution

accepted_payload.body: Require every backend binding to emit durable, searchable audit records for reads, previews, approval-required stops, denials, clarifications, and any later approved execution. Audit payloads should include requested capability, bounded target scope, applied policy result, preview artifact reference when produced, and lineage fields for task_id, invocation_id, client_reference_id, and parent_invocation_id.
accepted_payload.rationale: Audit durability, searchability, and lineage propagation are explicit locked requirements and are central to governed stop conditions and browser-verifiable review flows.
accepted_payload.title: Bind all governed flows to durable searchable audit with lineage propagation

accepted_payload.body: Implement backend resolution bindings for project keys, issue keys, assignee references, workflow statuses, sprint references, release references, components, and link types before constructing Jira-facing requests. Missing, ambiguous, or unresolved references should return clarification or denial according to the reviewed input contracts instead of guessing defaults.
accepted_payload.rationale: Developer evidence formalizes backend-resolved inputs across most capabilities, and the baseline requires clarification when required inputs or target scope are missing or ambiguous.
accepted_payload.title: Bind entity resolution through backend catalogs before Jira-facing requests

accepted_payload.body: Define an internal backend adapter seam that encapsulates Jira-specific reads, assignee resolution, and post-approval execution operations. The public governed capability layer should call this seam indirectly so the implementation can swap native Jira REST and Atlassian MCP without changing the external governed contract or browser-visible behavior.
accepted_payload.rationale: Source evidence explicitly allows either native API or MCP behind the same governed contract, and developer evidence already distinguishes public governed capabilities from internal adapter operations.
accepted_payload.title: Keep an internal adapter seam for Jira-specific operations behind the governed contract

accepted_payload.body: Specify the initial Jira environment target and the secret reference contract for base URL and credentials or signing material. Bindings should reference secret handles only, never embedded tokens, and should support deployment-time configuration for Jira base URL and optional MCP endpoint without changing capability contracts.
accepted_payload.rationale: Integration evidence names connection points and secret posture, but the actual environment target and auth reference names are still unspecified and should be explicit before generation.
accepted_payload.title: Make Jira environment target and secret references explicit in backend bindings

pm_artifacts.title: Backend Binding Proposal: Jira Governed Fronting Gate Clean

accepted_payload.body: Verification should prove that bounded read capabilities return only actor-visible Jira context and do not broaden into raw or unbounded access. For single-issue inspection, evidence should show one issue at a time with bounded fields, comments, and optional history only when requested. For backlog context, evidence should show project-bounded, actor-visible results and confirm that missing project scope triggers clarification instead of guessed defaults. Verification should also confirm that raw Jira REST semantics, raw MCP tools, and raw JQL are not exposed through the browser UI or public capability contract.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline explicitly requires bounded read-only Jira context, actor-visible scope enforcement, clarification on missing inputs, and no raw tool exposure.
accepted_payload.title: Verify bounded read behavior on the public Jira context surface

accepted_payload.body: Verification should prove that write-adjacent capabilities produce a governed preview or request artifact and do not directly mutate Jira before approval conditions are satisfied. This should cover story preparation, incident bug preparation, customer-safe comment drafting, release-note drafting where applicable, assignee change requests, sprint move requests, workflow transition requests, and issue link requests. Evidence should show preview content or approval-ready request state, plus explicit proof that no Jira mutation occurred during the verification step.
accepted_payload.rationale: The locked requirements state that comments, stories, bugs, assignee changes, sprint moves, workflow transitions, issue links, and release notes must stop at preview or approval rather than silently execute.
accepted_payload.title: Verify preview-only posture for write-adjacent Jira capabilities

accepted_payload.body: Verification should prove that the governed surface asks targeted clarification questions whenever required scope, target identifiers, relationship type, assignee identity, release scope, or other mandatory business inputs are missing, ambiguous, or unresolved. Evidence should show that the system does not guess defaults for unsafe values and that clarification prompts identify the specific missing input needed to continue. Coverage should include at least one read flow and multiple write-adjacent flows with different ambiguity types, such as missing issue key, broad release issue set, ambiguous assignee reference, and unresolved cross-project link details.
accepted_payload.rationale: Clarification on missing required inputs is a locked requirement and is reinforced by the developer input contracts across capabilities.
accepted_payload.title: Verify clarification behavior when required business inputs are missing or ambiguous

accepted_payload.body: Verification should prove that governed action flows evaluate scope and approval policy before any mutation boundary is crossed, and that approval-required cases stop explicitly in a reviewable state. Evidence should show the difference between allowed preview generation, approval-required stop, and denied or restricted outcomes without leaking out-of-scope Jira data. For workflow transitions, sprint moves, assignee changes, and issue linking, verification should confirm that policy evaluation is visible in the outcome and that blocked or unclear cases do not proceed silently.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline requires actor-visible scope and approval policy enforcement on every governed action, with explicit stops where approval or policy blocks apply.
accepted_payload.title: Verify approval boundaries and explicit policy-driven stop states

accepted_payload.body: Verification should prove that previews, approval-required requests, and policy-driven stop conditions generate durable and searchable audit evidence. Evidence should capture what was requested, what Jira scope and policy were applied, what preview or stop outcome was produced, and why execution did or did not proceed. Verification should also confirm presence of lineage fields required by the baseline, including task identifier, invocation identifier, client reference identifier, and parent invocation identifier where applicable.
accepted_payload.rationale: Audit durability, searchability, and lineage fields are explicit locked requirements and are central to governed fronting credibility.
accepted_payload.title: Verify durable, searchable audit evidence and lineage for governed outcomes

accepted_payload.body: Verification should include a browser-verifiable scenario pack that demonstrates the governed fronting behavior end to end. At minimum, the pack should show bounded issue inspection, bounded backlog context retrieval, a write-adjacent preview flow, an approval-required stop flow, a clarification flow for missing inputs, and visible absence of raw Jira tools in the UI. The expectation is not only backend correctness but also clean browser evidence that the product presents Jira as business-oriented governed capabilities rather than raw operational tooling.
accepted_payload.rationale: The first-release requirement explicitly calls for clean browser UI verification of governed Jira fronting and lists the behaviors that must be demonstrated.
accepted_payload.title: Verify first-release browser UI evidence for governed fronting end to end

accepted_payload.body: Verification should map evidence to the locked public capability inventory and the baseline scenarios so coverage is explicit rather than ad hoc. Expectations should ensure both read capabilities are covered, and that each write-adjacent capability family has at least one representative verification path showing preview, clarification, approval stop, or policy evaluation as appropriate. Scenario coverage should especially include release notes from bounded issues, customer-safe comment drafting without posting, assignee change with identity resolution, sprint move with actor-visible board scope, workflow transition with approval stop, issue linking with cross-project policy checks, story preview with acceptance criteria, incident bug preview, single-issue context inspection, and bounded backlog context discovery.
accepted_payload.rationale: The baseline provides a concrete scenario set and canonical capability inventory, so verification expectations should preserve that coverage explicitly.
accepted_payload.title: Verify coverage against the locked capability inventory and scenario set

items.body: Verification should prove that bounded read capabilities return only actor-visible Jira context and do not broaden into raw or unbounded access. For single-issue inspection, evidence should show one issue at a time with bounded fields, comments, and optional history only when requested. For backlog context, evidence should show project-bounded, actor-visible results and confirm that missing project scope triggers clarification instead of guessed defaults. Verification should also confirm that raw Jira REST semantics, raw MCP tools, and raw JQL are not exposed through the browser UI or public capability contract.
items.rationale: The baseline explicitly requires bounded read-only Jira context, actor-visible scope enforcement, clarification on missing inputs, and no raw tool exposure.
items.title: Verify bounded read behavior on the public Jira context surface

items.body: Verification should prove that write-adjacent capabilities produce a governed preview or request artifact and do not directly mutate Jira before approval conditions are satisfied. This should cover story preparation, incident bug preparation, customer-safe comment drafting, release-note drafting where applicable, assignee change requests, sprint move requests, workflow transition requests, and issue link requests. Evidence should show preview content or approval-ready request state, plus explicit proof that no Jira mutation occurred during the verification step.
items.rationale: The locked requirements state that comments, stories, bugs, assignee changes, sprint moves, workflow transitions, issue links, and release notes must stop at preview or approval rather than silently execute.

Coverage should include at least one read flow and multiple write-adjacent flows with different ambiguity types, such as missing issue key, broad release issue set, ambiguous assignee reference, and unresolved cross-project link details.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can search bounded backlog context, inspect issue context, and prepare previews for stories, subtasks, incident bugs, customer-safe comments, release notes, assignee changes, sprint moves, workflow transitions, and issue links within the governed surface.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Can proceed directly with bounded read actions. For write-adjacent actions, should stop at preview or submit a governed request when approval is required by policy rather than executing changes directly.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Business user who works through the governed Jira fronting surface to inspect bounded Jira context and prepare Jira changes without direct access to raw Jira tools.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can view bounded Jira issue and backlog context limited to actor-visible fields, comments, history, and allowed project or board scope. Should not see raw JQL, raw REST or MCP tools, or unbounded issue export.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can review requests for governed Jira actions such as workflow transitions, sprint moves, assignee changes, issue links, or other write-adjacent operations that require explicit approval.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Acts as the approval boundary for policy-gated Jira actions, but the brief does not specify whether this authority is universal or varies by action type.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Business actor who reviews governed Jira execution requests and associated audit evidence before policy-gated changes proceed.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can inspect the prepared Jira change preview, relevant bounded context, policy signals, and audit evidence needed to approve or reject a governed request.

permission_intent_bindings.formalization_strategy: jira_requester bounded access to Jira Context Access should return bounded_result. Allow bounded, reviewable jira context access behavior for jira requester when the request stays within actor-visible scope; clarify missing critical context, deny raw export or unsupported mutation, and stop for approval before side effects. product_alignment.approval_posture_formalization: Read-only Jira context can proceed within bounded visibility limits. Write-adjacent actions must stop at preview or approval before execution, with policy checks and audit evidence recorded for governed requests.
product_alignment.governed_behavior_formalization: The product should return only bounded, actor-visible Jira context for reads; never expose raw Jira tools directly; prepare previews for write-adjacent actions; require explicit approval when policy requires execution; and ask clarifying questions when required inputs are missing.

required_anip_support: Support bounded aggregation over Jira issues as a governed capability.
Enforce non-publishing, non-mutation behavior for release-note preparation.
Require explicit bounded scope before generation.
Capture audit evidence for source issue set and prepared output.
required_behaviors: The service requires a bounded issue set as input.
The service prepares release notes only and does not publish them.
The service does not mutate Jira as part of this flow.
If the issue set is too broad or unspecified, the service asks for clarification.
scenario_formalizations.scenario_title: Prepare release notes from bounded Jira issues without publishing

required_anip_support: Support governed drafting for communication artifacts.
Preserve preview-only behavior for write-adjacent comment flows.
Require sufficient source context before drafting.
Record audit evidence for prepared content and non-posting stop condition.
required_behaviors: The service prepares a comment preview only.
The comment is framed as customer-safe based on the provided context.
No Jira comment is posted automatically.
Missing context triggers clarification.
scenario_formalizations.scenario_title: Prepare a customer-safe Jira comment without posting it

required_anip_support: Support policy-gated relationship requests between Jira issues.
Make safe-stop and approval outcomes explicit.
Require concrete issue pair and relationship inputs.
Capture audit evidence for policy evaluation and final disposition.
required_behaviors: The service requires a declared relationship type for the link request.
Cross-project policy is checked before any link is created.
Blocked or unclear cases stop explicitly rather than proceeding silently.
The decision is auditable.
scenario_formalizations.scenario_title: Request linking two Jira issues with cross-project policy checks

required_anip_support: Support identity resolution as part of governed change preparation.
Represent preview and approval as explicit workflow states.
Require clarification for ambiguous assignee references.
Record audit evidence for requested assignee, resolved identity, and approval outcome.
required_behaviors: The service resolves the assignee against backend Jira identity.
The service shows a preview before any reassignment is executed.
Ambiguous user references trigger clarification.
Approval and audit controls are applied to the change request.
scenario_formalizations.scenario_title: Request an assignee change with resolution, preview, approval, and audit

required_anip_support: Enforce board-scope visibility in governed planning actions.
Support multi-issue request preparation with explicit stop conditions.
Represent approval boundaries clearly to users.
Capture audit evidence for requested issue set, target scope, and policy outcome.
required_behaviors: The service limits sprint move requests to actor-visible board scope.
The service supports one or more issues in the request.
Approval is required when policy says so.
The request path is auditable before any execution.
scenario_formalizations.scenario_title: Request moving issues into a sprint with actor-visible board scope

required_anip_support: Model approval-required outcomes as first-class runtime states.
Support policy evaluation before write execution.
Expose explicit stop conditions to the user and reviewer.
Record audit evidence for request, policy result, and approval boundary.
required_behaviors: The service does not expose raw transition tools directly to the agent.
The service prepares a governed transition request.
When policy requires approval, the flow stops explicitly before execution.
The decision path is auditable.
scenario_formalizations.scenario_title: Request a workflow transition and stop when approval is required

required_anip_support: Support governed drafting flows that stop at preview.
Make required-input clarification explicit.
Preserve non-mutation guarantees in runtime behavior.
Capture audit evidence for prepared story content and stop condition.
required_behaviors: The service returns a story preview with acceptance criteria.
The service does not create the story in Jira.
If the request lacks enough context, the service asks targeted follow-up questions.
The preview is suitable for review before any later creation step.
scenario_formalizations.scenario_title: Prepare a Jira story preview with acceptance criteria

required_anip_support: Support prepare-only write-adjacent flows with explicit non-mutation posture.
Represent preview output as a governed artifact.
Require clarification for missing mandatory bug inputs.
Record audit evidence that a preview was prepared but not executed.
required_behaviors: The service prepares a bug preview only.
No Jira issue is created automatically.
Missing required inputs trigger clarification instead of guessed values.
The preview can be reviewed before any later approval or creation flow.

required_anip_support: Support bounded single-record reads as first-class business capabilities.

The service does not broaden into unbounded issue export.

required_anip_support: Present bounded Jira business capabilities instead of raw tool access.
Enforce actor-visible read scope at runtime.

required_behaviors: The service returns bounded backlog context only for an allowed Jira project.
The service does not expose raw REST, MCP tools, or raw JQL to the agent.
If project scope is missing, the service asks for clarification instead of guessing.

non_goal_guards.non_goal: Do not support unbounded Jira reads such as broad issue export or unrestricted project-wide search results.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: PM review of representative read flows shows users receive actor-visible issue or backlog context through governed capability surfaces, and no raw tool or unbounded query path is presented.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Agents can access Jira through bounded business capabilities for issue and backlog context without exposure to raw REST, MCP tools, or unbounded JQL-style querying.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Verification of issue-context and backlog-context scenarios shows returned data is scoped, actor-visible, and bounded to allowed project or issue context.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Read-only Jira access returns bounded, actor-visible context rather than unrestricted issue export or hidden field access.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: PM review across representative actions such as comments, subtasks, stories, incident bugs, assignee changes, sprint moves, workflow transitions, issue links, and release notes shows the product produces a preview or approval request rather than silently executing changes.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Write-adjacent Jira actions stop at preview or explicit approval boundaries before any mutation is carried out.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Negative-path review shows incomplete or policy-sensitive requests trigger visible clarification or denial behavior with no inferred mutation-ready output.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: When required inputs are missing or policy checks block progress, the product asks for clarification or stops explicitly instead of guessing.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: PM or compliance review can inspect audit artifacts for representative governed actions and confirm they capture the request, preview or approval boundary, and resulting decision state.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Approval-gated and preview-based Jira flows produce reviewable audit evidence for what was requested, what was shown, and why execution did or did not proceed.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can search bounded backlog context, inspect issue context, and prepare previews for stories, subtasks, incident bugs, customer-safe comments, release notes, assignee changes, sprint moves, workflow transitions, and issue links within the governed surface.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Can proceed directly with bounded read actions. For write-adjacent actions, should stop at preview or submit a governed request when approval is required by policy rather than executing changes directly.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Business user who works through the governed Jira fronting surface to inspect bounded Jira context and prepare Jira changes without direct access to raw Jira tools.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can view bounded Jira issue and backlog context limited to actor-visible fields, comments, history, and allowed project or board scope. Should not see raw JQL, raw REST or MCP tools, or unbounded issue export.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can review requests for governed Jira actions such as workflow transitions, sprint moves, assignee changes, issue links, or other write-adjacent operations that require explicit approval.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Acts as the approval boundary for policy-gated Jira actions, but the brief does not specify whether this authority is universal or varies by action type.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Business actor who reviews governed Jira execution requests and associated audit evidence before policy-gated changes proceed.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can inspect the prepared Jira change preview, relevant bounded context, policy signals, and audit evidence needed to approve or reject a governed request.

product_alignment.approval_posture_formalization: Read-only Jira context can proceed within bounded visibility limits. Write-adjacent actions must stop at preview or approval before execution, with policy checks and audit evidence recorded for governed requests.
product_alignment.governed_behavior_formalization: The product should return only bounded, actor-visible Jira context for reads; never expose raw Jira tools directly; prepare previews for write-adjacent actions; require explicit approval when policy requires execution; and ask clarifying questions when required inputs are missing.

The service prepares release notes only and does not publish them.
The service does not mutate Jira as part of this flow.
If the issue set is too broad or unspecified, the service asks for clarification.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can search bounded backlog context, inspect issue context, and prepare previews for stories, subtasks, incident bugs, customer-safe comments, release notes, assignee changes, sprint moves, workflow transitions, and issue links within the governed surface.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Can proceed directly with bounded read actions. For write-adjacent actions, should stop at preview or submit a governed request when approval is required by policy rather than executing changes directly.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Business user who works through the governed Jira fronting surface to inspect bounded Jira context and prepare Jira changes without direct access to raw Jira tools.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can view bounded Jira issue and backlog context limited to actor-visible fields, comments, history, and allowed project or board scope. Should not see raw JQL, raw REST or MCP tools, or unbounded issue export.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can review requests for governed Jira actions such as workflow transitions, sprint moves, assignee changes, issue links, or other write-adjacent operations that require explicit approval.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Acts as the approval boundary for policy-gated Jira actions, but the brief does not specify whether this authority is universal or varies by action type.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Business actor who reviews governed Jira execution requests and associated audit evidence before policy-gated changes proceed.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can inspect the prepared Jira change preview, relevant bounded context, policy signals, and audit evidence needed to approve or reject a governed request.

Allow bounded, reviewable jira context access behavior for jira requester when the request stays within actor-visible scope; clarify missing critical context, deny raw export or unsupported mutation, and stop for approval before side effects. product_alignment.approval_posture_formalization: Read-only Jira context can proceed within bounded visibility limits. Write-adjacent actions must stop at preview or approval before execution, with policy checks and audit evidence recorded for governed requests.
product_alignment.governed_behavior_formalization: The product should return only bounded, actor-visible Jira context for reads; never expose raw Jira tools directly; prepare previews for write-adjacent actions; require explicit approval when policy requires execution; and ask clarifying questions when required inputs are missing.

required_anip_support: Support bounded aggregation over Jira issues as a governed capability.
Enforce non-publishing, non-mutation behavior for release-note preparation.
Require explicit bounded scope before generation.
Capture audit evidence for source issue set and prepared output.
required_behaviors: The service requires a bounded issue set as input.
The service prepares release notes only and does not publish them.
The service does not mutate Jira as part of this flow.
If the issue set is too broad or unspecified, the service asks for clarification.
scenario_formalizations.scenario_title: Prepare release notes from bounded Jira issues without publishing

required_anip_support: Support governed drafting for communication artifacts.
Preserve preview-only behavior for write-adjacent comment flows.
Require sufficient source context before drafting.
Record audit evidence for prepared content and non-posting stop condition.
required_behaviors: The service prepares a comment preview only.
The comment is framed as customer-safe based on the provided context.
No Jira comment is posted automatically.
Missing context triggers clarification.
scenario_formalizations.scenario_title: Prepare a customer-safe Jira comment without posting it

required_anip_support: Support policy-gated relationship requests between Jira issues.

Capture audit evidence for policy evaluation and final disposition.
required_behaviors: The service requires a declared relationship type for the link request.
Cross-project policy is checked before any link is created.
Blocked or unclear cases stop explicitly rather than proceeding silently.
The decision is auditable.

The service shows a preview before any reassignment is executed.
Ambiguous user references trigger clarification.
Approval and audit controls are applied to the change request.

required_anip_support: Enforce board-scope visibility in governed planning actions.

Capture audit evidence for requested issue set, target scope, and policy outcome.
required_behaviors: The service limits sprint move requests to actor-visible board scope.

required_anip_support: Model approval-required outcomes as first-class runtime states.

Record audit evidence for request, policy result, and approval boundary.
required_behaviors: The service does not expose raw transition tools directly to the agent.

When policy requires approval, the flow stops explicitly before execution.

required_anip_support: Support governed drafting flows that stop at preview.

required_anip_support: Support prepare-only write-adjacent flows with explicit non-mutation posture.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: PM review of representative read flows shows users receive actor-visible issue or backlog context through governed capability surfaces, and no raw tool or unbounded query path is presented.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Agents can access Jira through bounded business capabilities for issue and backlog context without exposure to raw REST, MCP tools, or unbounded JQL-style querying.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Verification of issue-context and backlog-context scenarios shows returned data is scoped, actor-visible, and bounded to allowed project or issue context.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Read-only Jira access returns bounded, actor-visible context rather than unrestricted issue export or hidden field access.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: PM review across representative actions such as comments, subtasks, stories, incident bugs, assignee changes, sprint moves, workflow transitions, issue links, and release notes shows the product produces a preview or approval request rather than silently executing changes.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Write-adjacent Jira actions stop at preview or explicit approval boundaries before any mutation is carried out.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Negative-path review shows incomplete or policy-sensitive requests trigger visible clarification or denial behavior with no inferred mutation-ready output.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: When required inputs are missing or policy checks block progress, the product asks for clarification or stops explicitly instead of guessing.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: PM or compliance review can inspect audit artifacts for representative governed actions and confirm they capture the request, preview or approval boundary, and resulting decision state.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Approval-gated and preview-based Jira flows produce reviewable audit evidence for what was requested, what was shown, and why execution did or did not proceed.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can search bounded backlog context, inspect issue context, and prepare previews for stories, subtasks, incident bugs, customer-safe comments, release notes, assignee changes, sprint moves, workflow transitions, and issue links within the governed surface.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Can proceed directly with bounded read actions. For write-adjacent actions, should stop at preview or submit a governed request when approval is required by policy rather than executing changes directly.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Business user who works through the governed Jira fronting surface to inspect bounded Jira context and prepare Jira changes without direct access to raw Jira tools.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can view bounded Jira issue and backlog context limited to actor-visible fields, comments, history, and allowed project or board scope. Should not see raw JQL, raw REST or MCP tools, or unbounded issue export.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can review requests for governed Jira actions such as workflow transitions, sprint moves, assignee changes, issue links, or other write-adjacent operations that require explicit approval.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Acts as the approval boundary for policy-gated Jira actions, but the brief does not specify whether this authority is universal or varies by action type.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Business actor who reviews governed Jira execution requests and associated audit evidence before policy-gated changes proceed.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can inspect the prepared Jira change preview, relevant bounded context, policy signals, and audit evidence needed to approve or reject a governed request.

product_alignment.approval_posture_formalization: Read-only Jira context can proceed within bounded visibility limits. Write-adjacent actions must stop at preview or approval before execution, with policy checks and audit evidence recorded for governed requests.
product_alignment.governed_behavior_formalization: The product should return only bounded, actor-visible Jira context for reads; never expose raw Jira tools directly; prepare previews for write-adjacent actions; require explicit approval when policy requires execution; and ask clarifying questions when required inputs are missing.

required_anip_support: Support bounded aggregation over Jira issues as a governed capability.
Enforce non-publishing, non-mutation behavior for release-note preparation.
Require explicit bounded scope before generation.
Capture audit evidence for source issue set and prepared output.
required_behaviors: The service requires a bounded issue set as input.
The service prepares release notes only and does not publish them.
The service does not mutate Jira as part of this flow.
If the issue set is too broad or unspecified, the service asks for clarification.

required_anip_support: Support governed drafting for communication artifacts.

The comment is framed as customer-safe based on the provided context.
No Jira comment is posted automatically.
Missing context triggers clarification.

required_anip_support: Support policy-gated relationship requests between Jira issues.

The decision is auditable.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: PM review of representative read flows shows users receive actor-visible issue or backlog context through governed capability surfaces, and no raw tool or unbounded query path is presented.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Agents can access Jira through bounded business capabilities for issue and backlog context without exposure to raw REST, MCP tools, or unbounded JQL-style querying.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Verification of issue-context and backlog-context scenarios shows returned data is scoped, actor-visible, and bounded to allowed project or issue context.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Read-only Jira access returns bounded, actor-visible context rather than unrestricted issue export or hidden field access.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: PM review across representative actions such as comments, subtasks, stories, incident bugs, assignee changes, sprint moves, workflow transitions, issue links, and release notes shows the product produces a preview or approval request rather than silently executing changes.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Write-adjacent Jira actions stop at preview or explicit approval boundaries before any mutation is carried out.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: Negative-path review shows incomplete or policy-sensitive requests trigger visible clarification or denial behavior with no inferred mutation-ready output.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: When required inputs are missing or policy checks block progress, the product asks for clarification or stops explicitly instead of guessing.

success_criteria_checks.evidence_expectation: PM or compliance review can inspect audit artifacts for representative governed actions and confirm they capture the request, preview or approval boundary, and resulting decision state.
success_criteria_checks.success_criterion: Approval-gated and preview-based Jira flows produce reviewable audit evidence for what was requested, what was shown, and why execution did or did not proceed.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can search bounded backlog context, inspect issue context, and prepare previews for stories, subtasks, incident bugs, customer-safe comments, release notes, assignee changes, sprint moves, workflow transitions, and issue links within the governed surface.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Can proceed directly with bounded read actions. For write-adjacent actions, should stop at preview or submit a governed request when approval is required by policy rather than executing changes directly.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Business user who works through the governed Jira fronting surface to inspect bounded Jira context and prepare Jira changes without direct access to raw Jira tools.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can view bounded Jira issue and backlog context limited to actor-visible fields, comments, history, and allowed project or board scope. Should not see raw JQL, raw REST or MCP tools, or unbounded issue export.

actor_expectations.action_formalization: Can review requests for governed Jira actions such as workflow transitions, sprint moves, assignee changes, issue links, or other write-adjacent operations that require explicit approval.
actor_expectations.approval_formalization: Acts as the approval boundary for policy-gated Jira actions, but the brief does not specify whether this authority is universal or varies by action type.
actor_expectations.summary_formalization: Business actor who reviews governed Jira execution requests and associated audit evidence before policy-gated changes proceed.
actor_expectations.visibility_formalization: Can inspect the prepared Jira change preview, relevant bounded context, policy signals, and audit evidence needed to approve or reject a governed request.

product_alignment.approval_posture_formalization: Read-only Jira context can proceed within bounded visibility limits. Write-adjacent actions must stop at preview or approval before execution, with policy checks and audit evidence recorded for governed requests.
product_alignment.governed_behavior_formalization: The product should return only bounded, actor-visible Jira context for reads; never expose raw Jira tools directly; prepare previews for write-adjacent actions; require explicit approval when policy requires execution; and ask clarifying questions when required inputs are missing.

business_constraints.assistant_drafted_requirements_summary:
Expose Jira through governed business capabilities rather than raw tools: The product shall present Jira as a bounded ANIP capability surface for agents and browser users. It shall not expose raw Jira REST endpoints, raw MCP tools, or unrestricted low-level operations directly. Capability entry points shall use business-oriented intents such as issue context, backlog context, and governed change requests.
Allow only bounded read-only Jira context: The product shall support read-only access to Jira issue context and backlog context within allowed Jira scope. Returned data shall be limited to actor-visible, bounded business context rather than unbounded exports or arbitrary query access. The system shall prevent raw JQL exposure and other mechanisms that could bypass bounded retrieval.
Gate all write-adjacent Jira actions at preview or approval: For write-adjacent Jira actions, the product shall stop before direct mutation unless an explicit approval path is satisfied. This applies to comments, subtasks, stories, incident bugs, assignee changes, sprint moves, workflow transitions, issue links, and release notes. The system shall return a preview or approval-ready request rather than silently executing the Jira change.
Require clarification when required inputs are missing: When a requested Jira capability lacks required inputs, policy context, or target scope, the product shall ask for clarification instead of guessing defaults or inferring unsafe values. Clarification prompts should identify the missing business input needed to continue the governed flow.
Enforce actor-visible scope and approval policy on every governed action: The product shall evaluate each read and write-adjacent request against actor-visible Jira scope and applicable approval policy before returning results or advancing a request. Where policy requires approval, the system shall stop at the approval boundary and present the request in a reviewable form. Where scope or policy denies access, the system shall not reveal out-of-scope Jira data.
Record audit evidence for previews, approvals, and governed stops: The product shall record audit evidence for write-adjacent previews, approval-required requests, and policy-driven stop conditions. Audit evidence should capture enough context to show what was requested, what Jira scope and policy were applied, what preview was produced, and why execution did or did not proceed.
Support clean browser UI verification of governed Jira fronting: The first release shall support browser-verifiable flows that demonstrate the governed fronting behavior end to end. Verification should show that users can access bounded Jira context, receive previews for write-adjacent actions, encounter approval stops where required, and observe that raw Jira tools are not exposed through the UI.

shape.name: Jira Governed Fronting Public Surface
notes: Public governed capability surface only; internal Jira API, MCP, adapter, and execution details are intentionally excluded.
Read capabilities return bounded actor-visible context.
Write-adjacent capabilities prepare previews or requests and stop for approval when policy requires.
Clarification on missing required inputs is treated as part of the governed contract.

services.name: Jira Context Service
responsibilities: Provide bounded backlog and issue context to agents
Enforce actor-visible scope on projects, issues, comments, and history
Prevent exposure of raw JQL, unbounded export, or raw Jira tool semantics
Return clarification when required read inputs are missing or ambiguous
services.role: public governed read surface for bounded Jira context

services.name: Jira Governed Action Service
responsibilities: Prepare Jira action previews without directly exposing mutation tools
Request governed Jira mutations that require approval or policy checks
Record audit evidence for previews, requests, approvals, and stop conditions
Enforce clarification stops when required inputs are missing
Apply cross-project and actor-visible policy constraints before any mutation boundary is crossed
